question_text: Two objects are placed on a balance scale. Object A has a mass of 5 kg and Object B has a mass of 3 kg. Both are on Earth where g = 10 N/kg. Which object pushes down with greater force on the scale, and by how many newtons?

options.label: A
options.text: Object A pushes with 20 N more force

options.label: B
options.text: Object A pushes with 50 N more force

options.label: C
options.text: Object B pushes with 30 N more force

options.label: D
options.text: Both push with equal force because they are on the same scale

explanation: Object A's weight = 5 × 10 = 50 N. Object B's weight = 3 × 10 = 30 N. The difference is 50 − 30 = 20 N, but the question asks which pushes down MORE, so Object A pushes with 20 N more force than Object B. Wait—let me recalculate: 50 N − 30 N = 20 N more. The correct answer should reflect this difference. Object A pushes down with 20 N more force than Object B.
